article_meta.title: ‘President Trump,” reports NPR, “says he hasn’t decided whether he’ll launch a military strike on Iran.” This is a remar

reasoning: The model correctly focused on the author’s own prose, flagged multiple genuine biased phrases, and applied largely appropriate bias-type labels. No quoted or attributed language was misattributed as author bias. It missed a couple of prominent loaded phrases but overall showed strong compliance.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are in the author’s narrative voice; no quotes or reproduced statements were flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each highlighted phrase reflects clear subjective framing, loaded word choice, or sensationalism in the author’s prose.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed some notable biased phrasings (e.g., ‘timorously acquiesce,’ ‘impotence,’ ‘slow-motion disaster’).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are generally fitting (sensationalism, spin, word choice). A few could arguably be labeled more precisely (e.g., ‘remarkable’ as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), but none are clearly wrong.
